_description: Foundational resource catalog generalizing the traditional item master to encompass physical goods, services, financial instruments, information assets, and capacity. Independently deployable without cross-domain dependencies.
_organizations_note: Organizations defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.
_tenants_note: Tenants defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.
_users_note: Users defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.

description.en: Foundational resource catalog generalizing the traditional item master to encompass physical goods, services, financial instruments, information assets, and capacity resources.
Core resource catalog operations: CRUD on resources, categories, and classification. Publishes capacity utilization events.
Enumeration of resource lifecycle statuses: Active, Discontinued, Pending, Blocked
Enumeration of depreciation calculation methods: StraightLine, DecliningBalance, UnitsOfProduction
Abstract base for anything the enterprise owns, uses, consumes, produces, assigns, or accounts for. Never instantiated directly.
Physical things with weight, volume, and tracking needs. Intermediate abstract universal.
Tradeable inventory goods -- products for sale or purchase. The traditional item master record.
Long-lived equipment, property, infrastructure. Subject to depreciation and capitalization.
Manufacturing inputs and BOM components. Consumed in production, not sold directly.
Non-physical resources with optional expiry and renewal. Intermediate abstract universal.
Labor, consulting, SaaS subscriptions. Billed by unit (hourly, daily, fixed).
Documents, datasets, intellectual property. May have access controls and version history.
Tradable or held-to-maturity instruments: bonds, securities, derivatives, notes. Excludes receivables and payables.
Software licenses, patent rights, franchise agreements. Governed by seat counts and terms.
Available capacity that can be allocated and consumed. Intermediate abstract universal.
Person-hours or FTE allocation. The how-much-people-time-is-available measure.
Machine-hours or production slots. Constrains manufacturing throughput.
Warehouse space, cloud storage quotas. Constrains inventory and data volume.
Classification category for resources. Supports recursive hierarchy via aggregation relator.

universals._comment: === Artifact output universal: Resource Inventory Summary ===

description.en: Aggregated resource inventory data by catalog category.
Classifies a resource into a catalog category. A resource may belong to one primary category.
Recursive category hierarchy. Aggregation because child categories survive parent reorganization.

property_definitions._comment: === erp-resource base properties (inherited by all specializations) ===

description.en: Unique catalog code (e.g., RES-00142)
Human-readable name, multilingual
Extended description for catalog display
Lifecycle status controlling availability
Base UOM for this resource (e.g., each, kg, hour, license)
Weight per unit. Used for shipping and logistics.
Volume per unit. Used for storage and packing.
Whether individual units are physically tracked (via serial, lot, or RFID)
Stock-keeping unit code for inventory systems
Can this item be purchased from vendors?
Can this item be sold to customers?
Standard (expected) cost per unit. Used for variance analysis.
Default selling price before discounts
Whether each unit has a unique serial number
Whether units are tracked by production lot/batch
Shelf life in days. Zero or absent means no expiry.
Date the asset was acquired or placed in service
Expected useful life for depreciation calculation (in seconds)
Estimated residual value at end of useful life
Method used to calculate periodic depreciation
Minimum quantity for vendor purchase orders
Typical lead time in days from order to receipt
Date when the resource expires or must be renewed. Absent means perpetual.
Description of renewal conditions, terms, and notice periods
Billing granularity: hourly, daily, monthly, fixed
Default rate per billing unit before contract-specific pricing
Format or media type (e.g., PDF, dataset, patent filing)
Sensitivity classification: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted
Nominal or face value of the instrument
Date when the instrument matures or is due
Type: Bond, Note, Equity, Derivative, Fund
Number of seats or units authorized. Zero means unlimited.
Type: Perpetual, Subscription, Trial, OEM
Total available capacity in the resource unit of measure
Currently unallocated capacity. Updated as allocations are made.
Computed: (total - available) / total * 100. Percentage of capacity in use.
Period granularity: daily, weekly, monthly
Full-time equivalent (e.g., 0.5 for half-time allocation)
Available machine-hours per production shift
Number of shifts per day the machine operates
Storage unit: pallet positions, cubic meters, terabytes
Whether the storage requires temperature/humidity control
Category code (e.g., RAW-ELEC, SVC-CONSULT)
Category name, multilingual
Extended description of the category scope
Resource category dimension for inventory aggregation
Count of resource instances per category
Sum of standard cost across physical items in the category
Tracks whether high-cost threshold has been crossed
Event emitted when capacity utilization exceeds threshold (e.g., 90%)
Event emitted when total standard cost in a category exceeds threshold
Computes utilization = (total - available) / total * 100. Fires when available capacity changes.

computations.formula: let t = total.value; let a = available.value; if t > 0.0 { (t - a) / t * 100.0 } else { 0.0 }

description.en: Aggregates resources by catalog category, providing counts and total standard cost across all resource types.
